mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month when policy is still easing and not at the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rank correlation stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS above trend indicates stronger nominal spending and revenue growth, which supports near-term equity earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises by at least 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth episodes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the conditional hit rate remains below 55% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening labor income and recession risk, which pressures cyclically exposed earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when unemployment is accelerating.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive more often than negative after UNRATE rises 0.3 pp in 3 months, or if the conditional rank IC is above zero in a sustained rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: An uptrend in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher following positive 3-month INDPRO momentum, or if the signal loses explanatory power in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is low, real policy rates are deeply negative and liquidity is accommodative, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is above 2% annualized and FEDFUNDS is below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of elevated CPI and low FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if either variable alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and RSAFS captures weakening housing activity and consumer demand, which signals slower growth ahead and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls month-over-month and RSAFS falls month-over-month in the same release window
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in HOUST and RSAFS are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if one series alone fully captures the effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor slowdown, a falling PAYEMS series combined with a rising UNRATE confirms payroll deterioration and rising recession risk, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines over 2 months while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS decline and UNRATE rise does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a demand rebound after slowdown, rising GDPC1 and PAYEMS together indicate improving real activity and household income, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 turns positive on the latest quarterly print and PAYEMS is rising on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint recovery in GDPC1 and PAYEMS does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal is not stronger than either proxy alone.
